limitations: Infer profile from evidence, not file name
source.expected_profile_note: People and talent profile; no formal people management should be inferred

conversations.title: Structured interview framework
conversations.summary: Designed behavioural interview questions, scoring anchors and bias controls.

evidence.claim: Created a repeatable interview process linked to role requirements and observable behaviours.
evidence.supporting_excerpt: Each question should map to one competency, include behavioural anchors and be scored before panel discussion.

evidence.claim: Introduced controls to reduce halo effect and unstructured judgement.
evidence.supporting_excerpt: Interviewers should score independently before calibration to avoid anchoring.

conversations.title: Candidate scorecard calibration
conversations.summary: Calibrated a candidate scorecard with hiring managers.

evidence.claim: Distinguished critical criteria from developable skills.
evidence.supporting_excerpt: Separate minimum requirements from capabilities that can be developed in the first six months.

conversations.title: Workforce capability gap analysis
conversations.summary: Mapped future capability needs and gaps.

evidence.claim: Mapped business priorities to current gaps and hiring or development actions.
evidence.supporting_excerpt: For each roadmap priority, identify required capability, current coverage, gap severity and response.

evidence.claim: Structured learning around work outcomes rather than course completion.
evidence.supporting_excerpt: A module is complete only when the learner applies the behaviour in a work task and receives feedback.

conversations.title: Development feedback for an employee
conversations.summary: Prepared developmental feedback grounded in observable behaviour.

evidence.claim: Framed feedback around specific behaviour, impact and a practical improvement plan.
evidence.supporting_excerpt: Describe the observed behaviour, explain its impact and agree one action to test.
evidence.counter_evidence: A single feedback example does not demonstrate formal people management.

evidence.claim: Proposed evidence-based mobility criteria covering capability, motivation and readiness.
evidence.supporting_excerpt: Mobility decisions should combine demonstrated capability, learning agility and readiness.

conversations.title: Recruitment funnel analysis
conversations.summary: Analysed hiring funnel conversion by stage.

evidence.claim: Separated volume issues from assessment quality issues.
evidence.supporting_excerpt: Analyse stage-by-stage before changing sourcing volume.

conversations.title: Employer branding copy
conversations.summary: Refined employer branding copy for a technical audience.

evidence.claim: Translated employee experience themes into candidate-facing messaging.
evidence.supporting_excerpt: Lead with the work people will do, autonomy and growth support.

conversations.title: Headcount budgeting question
conversations.summary: Asked for a headcount budget template with little context.

evidence.supporting_excerpt: Create a headcount budget for next year.
evidence.counter_evidence: No cost assumptions, hiring plan or scenario validation were provided.

evidence.claim: Structured a coaching discussion around reflection and commitments.
evidence.supporting_excerpt: Define the desired outcome, explore options and choose one observable commitment.
evidence.counter_evidence: This does not demonstrate ownership of the manager's performance.

conversations.title: Hiring process retrospective
conversations.summary: Identified bottlenecks in a slow hiring process.

evidence.claim: Identified approval delays, unclear ownership and inconsistent feedback.
evidence.supporting_excerpt: Measure time lost, assign one decision owner and define a feedback deadline.

conversations.title: Team performance review request
conversations.summary: Asked AI to draft reviews without behavioural evidence.

evidence.supporting_excerpt: Write performance reviews for my team based on role expectations.
evidence.counter_evidence: No specific observations, objectives, outcomes or direct management evidence were supplied.
